academic_profile: 3.95 unweighted GPA, top 3% of her class. 1280 PSAT, taking the SAT for the first time in August, hoping for 1350+. Her school offers 8 AP classes and she's taken or is taking 5 of them (Bio, Chem, Lang, US History, Calc AB).
academic_strengths: Science, especially biology and chemistry. Disciplined studier, never misses an assignment.
academic_weaknesses: Test-taking nerves. Her PSAT underestimates her, she does better on classwork than timed tests. Limited AP options at her school.
extracurriculars: Works 15 hours a week at H-E-B, watches her two younger siblings most evenings, National Honor Society, started a free tutoring program for freshmen at her school, church youth group
interests: Biology, medicine, she's wanted to be a nurse or maybe a doctor since her abuela was sick. Reads everything about health careers.
personality: Responsible beyond her years. Quiet in class but the person everyone asks for help. Doesn't like asking for things for herself.
teacher_quote: Her AP Bio teacher told me she's the best student she's had in ten years and that it would be a tragedy if she doesn't go to a four-year school.
parent1_profession: Front desk manager at a hotel near the airport
relocation: She'd go where the money works, but Texas would be easier for family
assets: Rent. No savings to speak of, small emergency fund.
college_budget: $8,000 per year would be a real stretch. Honestly less would be better.
financial_special: Alejandra would be the first in our family to go to college. I can't help much financially but I will do whatever it takes.
geographic_preference: Texas preferred, but open if the aid is real
must_haves: Strong biology or pre-health program, real financial aid, support for first-generation students
deal_breakers: Loans I have to co-sign. Anything that leaves her buried in debt.
priority_other: Her counselor has 600 students. Nobody has time to help her figure this out. That's why I'm here.
additional_context: She's top 3% of her class which I heard means automatic admission to Texas public universities. Someone at church mentioned QuestBridge but we don't know if it's real or how it works. She's a hard worker and deserves a real shot.
